speaker: speaker2
text: I can hardly make ends meet these days .

speaker: speaker1
text: same here . I have't brought any new clothes for three months .

speaker: speaker2
text: my income remains the same but prices just continue to rise .

speaker: speaker1
text: true . The news says that there is a general increase in the price of daily necessities .

speaker: speaker2
text: definitely . I have to spend 300 yuan more on food every month .

speaker: speaker1
text: the biggest price jump is for pork , so most people have to turn to chicken .

speaker: speaker1
text: inflation is more than just the rising prices , I guess . But the central bank is warning about the risk of inflation .

speaker: speaker2
text: has the government taken some measures to curb it ?

speaker: speaker1
text: yes . The government has banned price hikes in some key industries like energy .

speaker: speaker2
text: but I'm afraid the price hikes will spill over from food into other areas of the economy .

speaker: speaker1
text: you said it . I think we should tighten our budget and cut our spending .

speaker: speaker1
text: for a start , we can take the public transport to work . You know , running a car is a big expense .
